scenario: A company uses Microsoft Dataverse. You want to integrate with Microsoft Azure by calling an Azure Function that has an HTTP trigger. You need to pass a custom JSON payload synchronously to the Azure function when a row is created in a Dataverse table.
q: How should you build the integration? Select the appropriate options from the drop-down menus.

rows.s: To pass the data from Dataverse to Azure.
To define the endpoint and authentication details for the Azure Function.
To connect to the endpoint in Azure.

why: Build a Dataverse PLUG-IN that fires when a row is created; the plug-in passes a custom JSON payload to Azure synchronously. Use a WEBHOOK to define the Azure Function endpoint URL and its authentication (key/header). In the plug-in code, use the IServiceEndpointNotificationService interface to connect to the registered endpoint (webhook) and push the custom JSON object to the Azure Function. A Custom API is for calling INTO Dataverse, and Azure Service Bus is asynchronous - neither passes a synchronous custom payload out on a create event.
